_nist: AC-12 (session termination), SC-23 (session authenticity), IA-2 (authentication), AC-7 (account lockout)
_usage: Import via Keycloak admin UI or: curl -X POST KC_URL/admin/realms -H 'Authorization: Bearer TOKEN' -d @realm-export.json
_session_comment: SSO Session timeouts — NIST AC-12 session termination controls
_ssoSessionIdleTimeout_why: WHY: 30-minute idle timeout. NIST AC-12 requires session termination after inactivity. 1800s = 30min — standard for non-privileged users.
_ssoSessionMaxLifespan_why: WHY: 10-hour max session lifespan. Even active sessions must re-authenticate. Prevents indefinite sessions that bypass token revocation.
_ssoSessionIdleTimeoutRememberMe_why: WHY: If remember-me is ever enabled, restrict idle to 1 hour. More restrictive than standard session.
_ssoSessionMaxLifespanRememberMe_why: WHY: Max 24 hours even with remember-me enabled. Prevents multi-week persistent sessions.
_accessTokenLifespan_why: WHY: Access tokens expire in 5 minutes. Short-lived access tokens limit blast radius of token theft. Application must use refresh token to get new access token.
_accessCodeLifespan_why: WHY: Authorization codes expire in 60 seconds. Prevents code interception and replay attacks (SC-23 session authenticity).
_rememberMe_why: WHY: Remember-me disabled. Creates long-lived sessions that bypass AC-12 timeout controls. Users must re-authenticate per session.
_bruteforce_comment: Brute force protection — NIST AC-7 (unsuccessful login attempts)
_bruteForceProtected_why: WHY: Required. Without brute force protection, password spray and credential stuffing attacks run uninhibited. NIST AC-7 requires lockout after failed attempts.
_permanentLockout_why: WHY: Permanent lockout creates DoS vectors (attacker locks all accounts). Use temporary lockout with escalating waits instead.
_failureFactor_why: WHY: Lock after 5 failed attempts. Allows for typos but stops spray attacks that typically use >10 attempts per account.
_waitIncrementSeconds_why: WHY: First lockout = 30-second wait. Exponential backoff punishes repeated failures more harshly.
_maxFailureWaitSeconds_why: WHY: Maximum lockout period = 15 minutes. Long enough to stop automated attacks, short enough to not create DoS.
passwordPolicy: length(12) and upperCase(1) and lowerCase(1) and digits(1) and specialChars(1) and notUsername and notEmail and passwordHistory(5)
_passwordPolicy_why: WHY: NIST SP 800-63B minimum 8 chars; 12-char minimum provides better entropy. Mixed case + digits + special chars. No reuse of last 5 passwords. NotUsername/notEmail prevent trivial guessing.

requiredActions.name: Configure OTP
requiredActions._why: WHY: All new users must configure TOTP before first login. Makes MFA mandatory, not optional. NIST IA-2 requires MFA for all access to sensitive systems.

requiredActions.name: Verify Email
requiredActions._why: WHY: Verified email required before access. Prevents account creation with non-existent emails and supports account recovery.

requiredActions.name: Update Password
requiredActions._why: WHY: Not a default action — used for forced resets on compromised accounts or policy changes. Keep enabled so admins can trigger.

_otpPolicyType_why: WHY: TOTP (time-based) over HOTP (counter-based). TOTP codes expire every 30s; HOTP codes are valid until used — TOTP has shorter validity window.
_otpPolicyAlgorithm_why: WHY: SHA1 is standard for TOTP per RFC 6238. SHA256/SHA512 are more secure but not universally supported by authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Authy, Microsoft Authenticator).
_otpPolicyDigits_why: WHY: 6-digit codes are RFC 6238 standard and universally supported. 8-digit provides marginal security improvement at cost of usability.
_otpPolicyPeriod_why: WHY: 30-second windows are RFC 6238 standard. Shorter windows cause usability problems; longer windows extend the attack window for intercepted codes.
_otpPolicyLookAheadWindow_why: WHY: Allow 1 window drift (30 seconds each direction) to handle clock skew. Wider windows allow more code interception attempts.
_ssl_comment: SSL required — NIST SC-8 (transmission confidentiality) — set externally via reverse proxy
_sslRequired_why: WHY: Require SSL for external connections. Session tokens in transit must be protected. If behind a TLS-terminating proxy, set to external.
